name: Brindha Selvadurai
title: Director and Co-Founder
bio: Brindha Selvadurai is director and co-founder of Hatch Works, Sri Lanka’s pioneering startup hub. An entrepreneur and ecosystem builder, she has helped establish the country’s startup ecosystem, supporting more than 1,000 entrepreneurs and creating pathways for women and underserved founders to access capital, mentorship and growth opportunities. Using the learnings from her fellowship, Selvadurai plans to develop a scalable vocational training ecosystem that connects young people to skills, apprenticeships and employment, creating a model that can strengthen economic opportunity and workforce resilience across Sri Lanka and the wider region.

name: Keerthi Kodithuwakku
title: Chief Executive Officer
bio: Keerthi Kodithuwakku is the chief executive officer and co founder of Jendo Innovations, a health technology startup developing Sri Lanka’s first non invasive, AI powered device for early cardiovascular disease detection, with patents granted in the U.S. and Japan. He has led the company’s growth from a university research idea to a globally recognized, multi award winning med tech venture and advanced clinical AI research across Sri Lanka. Following his fellowship, he plans to establish a South Asian Regulatory Framework for AI Medical Devices to streamline innovation, strengthen regional standards and accelerate safe adoption of AI in health care.

name: Marion Lihini Fernando
title: Attorney-at-Law and Co-Founding Partner
bio: Lihini Fernando is attorney-at-law and co-founding partner at Velox Partners, a Sri Lankan law firm and legal process outsourcing company providing tech-enabled legal services across Asia and Australia. A politician, a member of the Moratuwa Municipal Council and founding member of NextGen Sri Lanka, she has been a leading advocate for women’s representation in politics and the rule of law in her country. She also has contributed to policy reform and political training initiatives led by the International Republican Institute and Westminster Foundation for Democracy. On fellowship, Fernando plans to establish community-based legal aid centers to provide free legal aid to underprivileged individuals who lack access to formal avenues of justice. She also intends to advance initiatives focused on women’s empowerment and increasing women’s participation and representation in politics.

name: Nevindaree Premarathne
title: Founder & CEO
bio: Nevindaree Premarathne is the founder and chief executive officer of The Makers Global, an ed-tech company transforming STEM education through hands-on learning for children ages four to 16. Since 2022, The Makers has reached more than 5,000 students in Sri Lanka, Malaysia, Singapore, Saudi Arabia, Australia and Norway with STEM kits, online learning platforms and personalized coaching. Premarathne’s work has earned global awards and recognition. Following fellowship, she wants to expand The Makers’ STEM approach into school curriculums through fun, educational experiments.

name: Asiri Hewamalage
title: National Program Manager
bio: A medical doctor within Sri Lanka’s Ministry of Health, Hewamalage plans, coordinates, monitors and evaluates programs to improve the health of mothers and children by emphasizing early intervention for children with developmental disabilities. On fellowship she explored how the use of specifically designed video games can be deployed to counter negative stereotypes about people with disabilities. Her secondary objective involved exploring the influence of perinatal mental health on children’s developmental outcomes and investigating how early childhood development research can contribute to inclusive policy and practice.

name: Seroshi Nandasiri
title: Founder and Chair
bio: The social-services organization Nandasiri founded is dedicated to gender equality and women’s well-being. Through it she advises a network of 125 female entrepreneurs across Sri Lanka and another 8,000 through two sister organizations. On fellowship she learned about the female empowerment movement in the United States. For her fellowship project she plans to pivot and create a network of 5,000 fathers to model what she calls positive masculinities.

name: Dhananath Fernando
title: Chief Operating Officer
bio: As Chief Executive Officer of Advocata, an independent think tank, Fernando fosters economic development through free-market enterprise and has worked extensively on a project to reduce the high cost of menstrual hygiene products in Sri Lanka. He was the host of a weekly television program that interviewed politicians and was part of a team that taught vision- and hearing-impaired students how to swim. He wants to create an online, trilingual economics education platform to help his compatriots “make better economic decisions,” learn about debt restructuring, “overcome poverty and improve their quality of life.”

name: Heminda Jayaweera
title: Chief Operating Officer
bio: Heminda Jayaweera manages business development, technology, marketing and operations at the Sri Lankan Institute of Nanotechnology, with a focus on increasing Sri Lankan high- tech exports through research and development. He oversees the institute’s transformation from an applied research facility to a product design engineering company. This is a three- year project that will require a shift towards innovation-focused research and product design, the forging of new entrepreneurial relationships with local and global high-tech markets and the securement of government support for exporting high-tech products. On fellowship, Jayaweera researched open innovation-based approaches to increasing exports and driving local business that he can apply in Sri Lanka.

name: Himesh Fernando
title: Founder and CEO
bio: Himesh Fernando is founder and CEO of Chaka Suthra, a startup inspired by his fellowship program that aims to use innovative technologies to support a zero-waste, circular economy. One of the firm’s products is the Trash2Cash, a recycling platform that up-cycles used packaging into polyester yarn, recycled fabric, books, paper and other household products. Fernando previously served as head of innovation for Hemas Holdings, a Sri Lankan group of companies in health care, personal care, leisure and transportation. On his fellowship he developed a strategy to minimize plastic waste from entering landfills and the oceans.

name: Chevaan Daniel
title: Executive Group Director
bio: Chevaan Daniel has a diverse role as the youngest group director of his organization, whose portfolio includes companies in various sectors such as media, chemicals and energy. He oversees News 1st, Sri Lanka's largest independent news network of TV and radio stations and also spearheads business development in the organization's other businesses such as renewable energy, national security and infrastructure growth. Additionally, Daniel plays a leading role in the humanitarian efforts, speaking, for example, at the first United Nations World Humanitarian Summit. With a commitment to humanitarianism and cross-border collaboration, Daniel used his fellowship to meet with policymakers, emerging thought leaders and organizations dedicated to corporate social responsibility to inform his development of a U.S.-Sri Lanka leadership exchange program. He also hopes to co-produce a documentary exploring the history of Sri Lanka.

name: Dulith Herath
title: Founder and Chairman
bio: Devoted to providing world-class service to online shoppers, Dulith Herath spearheads Sri Lanka’s largest e-commerce organization. Established in 2003, Kapruka has positioned itself as a market leader and global presence, showcasing more than 10,000 products and 30 types of distinct services, as well as maintaining a workforce of more than 200 employees and robust partnerships with more than 300 renowned brand names. Herath also has established Kapruka’s philanthropic arm through the Round Off for Charity program, a charity fund supported by the contributions of more than 60% of Kapruka’s loyal consumer base to fund philanthropy across Sri Lanka. Capitalizing on 13 years of experience, Kapruka is launching Grasshoppers.com, a project aimed at empowering small businesses in developing nations with the tools needed to seamlessly transition onto the Internet. On fellowship, Herath studyied global e-commerce trends in the United States in order to inform the Grasshoppers project and introduce e-commerce to developing countries while supporting rural entrepreneurs and small- and medium e-commerce ventures across Asia.

name: Nayana Mawilmada
title: President - Property Group JKH
bio: As a driving force behind a $1.25 billion investment in Sri Lanka's first urban, rapid mass-transit system, Nayana Mawilmada strives to improve transportation and access to housing in Western Sri Lanka. He was one of the thought leaders behind Sri Lanka's Megapolis project, which lays out a roadmap for sustainable urban transformation of Sri Lanka's capital region, one of the Sri Lankan government's most ambitious initiatives. He has been a key advocate and spokesperson for sound urban development policy and planning in Sri Lanka and strives to ensure that the housing sector in the country is well positioned to provide affordable, sustainable and quality urban housing through market mechanisms. On fellowship, Mawilmada explored successful examples of urban housing policy enforcement as well as relevant innovations, coalition-building and the role of communication, advocacy groups and other stakeholders in the implementation of fair housing policies.

name: Subramaniam Eassuwaren
title: Vice Chairman
bio: Subramaniam Eassuwaren helps lead a third-generation family business specializing in the production of tea. Helping create the first carbon-neutral tea producer in the world, Eassuwaren was responsible for the company's adoption of sustainable practices into the business, and continues to play a role in supporting other Sri Lankan businesses to develop sustainability plans as a consultant through the Carbon Consulting Company. While on fellowship, Eassuwaren analyzed how organizations measure environmental impact, as well as how companies communicate their sustainability message to both their clients and their stakeholders. Eassuwaren's goal is to develop a toolkit that will support Sri Lankan agricultural businesses in the adoption, implementation and maintenance of sustainability in their business models, environmental footprint and human capital development.

name: Bhavani Fonseka
title: Senior Researcher
bio: As senior researcher and attorney at the prominent think tank, Centre for Policy Alternatives, Bhavani Fonseka leads a team of lawyers in several high level public interest litigation cases. Her cases have set precedents in the protection of human rights and addressing the Sri Lankan culture of impunity. Fonseka is passionate about the dangers of a single narrative regarding Sri Lanka's civil war and is determined to help the country accept diverse viewpoints. In addition to litigation, she advocates greater protection of human rights and works to advance international accountability, particularly as part of civil society efforts at the U.N. Human Rights Council that have been instrumental in the passing of recent resolutions on Sri Lanka. Fonseka used her fellowship to develop a comprehensive plan for transitional justice in her country, including national processes and mechanisms to foster democracy and reconciliation at this critical time. She explored and learned from examples, best practices and emerging issues related to truth, justice, reparations and reconciliation.

name: Nathan Piranavan Sivagananathan
title: Co-founder and Director
bio: Nathan Sivagananathan is a successful strategist, entrepreneur, investor and innovator. He currently runs a multi-family office in Singapore, covering investments in South and Southeast Asia that focus on renewable energy, agriculture, hospitality, leisure, retail and aqua culture. He also is the co-founder and director of Hatch, Sri Lanka’s premier co-worker space that advocates free-thinking and networking for all things start-up. Sivagananathan sits on various boards of public companies in insurance, financial services and investments. Previously he served as chief growth officer and board director at MAS Holdings, a leading apparel tech company. Sivagananathan has a passion for philanthropy and co-founded the Colours of Courage Trust and Trail to raise funds to uplift cancer treatment infrastructure in Sri Lanka. He walked the length of Sri Lanka in 2011 and again in 2016 to raise more than $14 million USD by harnessing the power of social media and crowdfunding to build cancer facilities in the northern and southern rural areas of the country lacking access to this critical medical care. He was a 2021 YPO Global Impact Award Nominee from South Asia for his commitment to equitable access in healthcare.

name: Harsha Purasinghe
title: Founder and CEO
bio: Harsha Purasinghe is an entrepreneur who has founded and led several technology firms, including Microimage and MiHCM. Since founding Microimage in 1995 at the age of 19, he has served as the CEO and built the company into a multinational firm that specializes in human capital management and mobile and digital convergence.  He later started MiHCM, a leading digital human capital management platform that operates across the Asia-Pacific region. Purasinghe is an expert on the future of work and the digital transformation of human resources. In addition to his business ventures, Purasinghe is active in building Sri Lanka’s social impact ecosystem as an investor and mentor. He is a board member of the Lanka Impact Investing Network and a member of the Ceylon Chamber of Commerce.

name: Ruwanthie de Chickera
title: Artistic Director
bio: Ruwanthie de Chickera is a founding member and artistic director of the Stages Theatre Group, one of Sri Lanka's most well-known and highly-respected theater groups. In the past 15 years, she has written and/or directed more than 20 plays, while lecturing widely on acting and directing. She also wrote the script for the international award winning comedy Machan, and has produced feature and short films.  De Chickera used her fellowship to explore community theater; theater and film festivals; actor training techniques; and the development of film and theater scripts.

name: Anuruddha Bandara Padeniya
title: Former President
bio: As one of only two pediatric neurologists in Sri Lanka, Dr. Anuruddha Padeniya plans, coordinates, and delivers patient care services in Sri Lanka's premier children's hospital, provides consultancy services to hospitals throughout the country, and trains and teaches postgraduate and undergraduate medical and paramedical professionals. In 2020, Padeniya was re-elected as the president of the Government Medical Officers Association, for the 10th year. While on fellowship, Padeniya learned about pediatric neurology and neurodisability service delivery systems, examined the role of the public and private sectors in healthcare delivery, studied healthcare financing, explored continuing medical education programs, and investigated healthcare sector occupation classifications and compensation practices.

name: Heshma Wignaraja
title: Director
bio: Born into a pioneering family of dance in Sri Lanka, Heshma Wignaraja continues the work and legacy of her grandparents, who brought traditional indigenous dance to the national and international stage beginning in the 1940s. The Chitrasena Dance Company is Sri Lanka's oldest and most prestigious dance company, having represented Sri Lanka internationally since 1952. In her dual roles as Artistic Director of the dance company and Director of the Board of its foundation, Wignaraja is responsible for choreographing, producing, and directing all productions as well as overseeing administrative staff and managing strategic planning for the foundation. While in the U.S. she explored modern dance choreography, the latest techniques in dance training, and effective management of dance schools.

name: Jayantha Theobald Fernando
title: Legal Advisor
bio: Responsible for legal and policy reform at ICTA, Jayantha Fernando is one of three leaders of his organization’s $83 million flagship program, the e-Sri Lanka Development Project. He was instrumental in driving key policy and legislative reforms, most notably the Electronic Transactions Act (2006), Computer Crimes Act (2007), Payment Devices Frauds Act (2006), and Payment & Settlement Systems Act (2005). While on fellowship, Fernando explored legal issues surrounding government use of data centers and cloud computing services; cyber security concerns resulting from increased government use of ICT; and American perspectives on internet governance.

name: Kanchana Dilrukshi Weerakoon
title: Founder/President
bio: Kanchana Weerakoon is an ornithologist, conservationist and practitioner and trainer for sustainable living. Weerakoon is founder and president of ECO-V, a non-profit organization dedicated to inspiring people to take small steps to protect the environment.  She also founded and leads Journeys for Climate Justice, an Australia-based non-profit tackling climate change issues in the Asia-Pacific region. Under her leadership, these organizations have introduced training programs to develop climate champions and sustainable leaders and to teach children how to connect with nature using their five senses. Kanchana was awarded as the Most Outstanding Young Person of the Year in 2002 in Sri Lanka. She trained as a member of the Climate Change Reality Leadership Corps with former U.S. Vice President Al Gore in 2013 and was a finalist for the Desmond Tutu Reconciliation Award in 2018.

name: Saliya Pieris
title: President's Counsel/ Chairman
organization: Law Chambers/ Human Rights Commission of Sri Lanka
bio: Saliya Pieris P.C. heads his Law Chambers, practicing in the areas of criminal law, public law and fundamental rights in the Appellate and Original Courts. From 2021 to 2023 he was president of the Bar Association of Sri Lanka. During his tenure, the Chennai-based Hindu Newspaper, India’s second largest English-language newspaper, described the Sri Lankan bar association as a voice of reason. In 2022 Sri Lanka's prestigious LMD Magazine selected Pieris as the Sri Lankan of the Year. From 2018 to 2020, he was the first chairman of the Office on Missing Persons and between 2015 and 2018, he was a member of the Human Rights Commission of Sri Lanka. Pieris is a lecturer in law and has published many papers and articles on law and politics. He holds a master’s degree in international business law from the University of London.

name: Harsha de Silva
title: Member of Parliament
bio: Since December 2018, Harsha de Silva has been the Minister of Economic Reforms and Public Distribution in the Sri Lankan Parliament. Prior  to this, he was the Deputy Minister of Policy Planning and Economic Development, and the former Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs. He remains a strong advocate for increased economic liberalization. An internationally recognized development economist, de Silva hosted a popular weekly TV talk show on economics and politics from 2006 until 2010. On his fellowship he examined perspectives on economic freedom in the U.S. economy; the political and economic decision-making process; and television programming focused on political and economic issues

name: Manori Gunawardena
title: Director
bio: Manori Gunawardena is responsible for developing a comprehensive, long-term plan of conservation management for Asian elephants in the North Central Province of Sri Lanka.  She has a particular interest in poverty alleviation, effective land-use, and strategies to ameliorate conflicting interests of humans and elephants. On her fellowship, she visited both national and state agencies to explore how donor and intergovernmental funding incorporates conservation/environmental requirements in disseminating aid.

name: Susie Perera de Silva
title: Deputy Director General (Public Health Service)
bio: Susie De Silva, a clinician working to enhance Sri Lanka's health care infrastructure, developed a framework for monitoring and evaluated a World Bank-funded national STD/AIDS control program. While on fellowship, Silva sought broad exposure to health care policy and planning issues, as well as to monitor and evaluate health systems in the U.S. Her interests were particularly in the areas of change management, development planning, benchmarking practices, and performance monitoring in the healthcare arena.

name: Eran Wickramaratne
title: State Minister of Finance
bio: Eran Wickramaratne has been the state minister of finance since 2017. He was previously deputy minister of public enterprises, and also deputy minister for highways, higher education and investment promotion. Wickramaratne is a former vice-president at Citibank, and director/CEO of the National Development Bank Sri Lanka. He was also founder and chairman of the Information Communication Agency of Sri Lanka, the apex government body on Information Technology. During his fellowship he met with U.S. colleagues to discuss ways to ensure corporate governance, create an investor-friendly environment, make technology affordable to all and make government more ethical. He also used his fellowship to make contacts in the U.S. on conflict resolution with the hope that he can play a role in moving his country toward peaceful coexistence.

name: Jayasuriya Chrishantha Weliamuna
title: High Commissioner
organization: Sri Lanka High Commission in Canberra
bio: Jayasuriya Chrishantha Weliamuna is the current appointed High Commissioner of Sri Lanka to the Commonwealth of Australia.  Past positions include an attorney-at-law to the Supreme Court and Court of Appeals, as well as chairman of the Presidential Task Force on Recovery of Stolen Assets. He is a board member of the Global Board of Transparency International, and the former executive director and chairman of Transparency International – Sri Lanka. He has worked on proposed legislation on the Auditor General, Right to Information and Constitutional Changes to guarantee accountability and good governance in Sri Lanka. Other positions include serving as a consultant to the World Bank on a country financial accountability study; as a project director at the Institute of Human Rights on protection of public resources during elections; and as director and supervisor of the Electronic Media Monitoring Project. He has lead civil society and lawyers movements to bring about political changes in the country. He is a regular contributor to media on governance, human rights and current affairs.

name: Kala Oshadhi Peiris de Costa
title: Executive Director
bio: As the director of a rural development organization, Kala Peiris de Costa is responsible for policy and decision making, project implementation, fundraising, and monitoring and evaluation. Her community involvement includes participating as a Member of the Boards of the Center for Women’s Research, the NGO Management Development Centre of Sri Lanka, and the National Apprentice and Industrial Training Authority. She received the National Youth Award in 1994 for Children’s Literature. Peiris de Costa, who holds an MBA, has previously worked as a consultant for a number of international and national organizations such CIDA, CARE International, Oxfam, and the Asia Foundation. On fellowship she looked at a variety of NGO initiatives and examined the role and impact of social entrepreneurs in the United States.

name: Paikiasothy Saravanamuttu
title: Executive Director
bio: Paikiasothy Saravanamuttu is executive director of the Centre for Policy Alternatives (CPA). An independent, nonpartisan, public policy center with a focus on peace and governance, CPA is the leading public policy center in Sri Lanka. Saravanamuttu's responsibilities include interfacing with government, opposition parties, the diplomatic community, civil society organizations and the media. He is one of the founders of the Centre for Monitoring Election Violence and of Transparency International’s Sri Lanka Chapter. He was the first recipient of the National Peace Council’s Sri Lanka Citizen Peace Award and in 2013 attended President Obama's High Level Civil Society Round Table. A member of the Foreign Policy Advisory Group to the Foreign Minister of Sri Lanka, previously served as the secretary of the Consultation Task Force on Reconciliation Mechanisms and as a member of the board of the Lakshman Kadirgamar Institute for International and Strategic Studies. He holds a PhD in international relations from the London School of Economics.

name: Nivard Ajith Leslie Cabraal
title: Former State Minister of Money & Capital Market and State Enterprise Reforms
bio: From 2006-2015, Cabral served as the governor of the Central Bank of Sri Lanka. Until 2005 Cabraal had a management consulting company (Cabraal Consulting Group) which specialized in corporate governance (strategic planning and mediation) and turnarounds. He has also served as president or board member of various organizations and institutions of a professional or regulatory nature

name: Nimal Samarakkody
title: Chairman
bio: Nimal Samarakkody is a business executive and the chairman of the board at S. A. Perera & Company, a diversified firm with interests in real estate, agriculture and textiles.

name: Chandra Jayaratne
title: Managing Director
bio: Chandra Jayaratne is a retired business executive who held senior leadership positions in the public and private sectors. He served as executive chairman of Willalhamer Real Estate Co. and group planning director of Ceylon Tobacco Company Ltd.

name: Hettipathirannahelge Dayananda Gunawardena
title: Chairman (retired)
bio: H. Dayananda Gunawardena was the chairman and founder of Prudential Holdings Group of Companies until its closure in 2010. He graduated from the University of Ceylon with a special degree in physics with subsidiary mathematics in 1968, and later in 1974 received a post graduate diploma in ports and shipping administration from the University of Wales Institute of Science and Technology (UWIST) Cardiff. He started his career as a lecturer in Sri Lankan Universities, but after a few years, decided to put his knowledge into actual practice. He served in the Ceylon Shipping Corporation, the national carrier of Sri Lanka, almost from its inception and was responsible for feasibility studies for containerization in the last quarter of 1979. Thereafter, he spent 11 years in the private sector with an initial year in Greece, followed by serving in prestigious companies such as John Keells Holdings and Delmege Forsyth & Company in Sri Lanka for a further 10 years. These two companies represented large shipping lines: P&O, OCCL, Mitsui OSK, Sea Consortium (container feeders in the sub-continent). He set up his own ventures in 1992, Prudential Holdings Group of Companies in shipping, logistics and freight management, automobiles, ICT and manufacturing woven labels using the latest technology (Electronic Looms) for the apparel industry. This company “Impressions” was sold to Avery Dennison Corporation, Pasadena, California, USA in 2005. He continued his association with the Prudential Shipping Group of Companies until November 2015. He is currently engaged in promoting investments to Sri Lanka and assisting The Nanotechnology Institute www.slintec.lk

name: Julius Asoka de Lanerolle
title: Former Consultant
bio: Asoka de Lanerolle is an advisor/consultant for business, trade and marketing. He is the former chairman of the Sri Lanka Standards Institute and the Export Development Board. He was a founder member of the Sri Lanka Institute of Marketing. De Lanerolle is a retired advisor for the United Nations Conference on Trade and Development and the International Trade Centre. He holds degrees from the University of Peradeniya (Sri Lanka) in Economics and Political Science.

name: Godfrey Gunatilleke
title: Director
organization: Sri Lanka Center for Development Studies
bio: Godfrey Gunatilleke graduated in English (1st Class Hons) from the University of Ceylon, 1948. He entered the Ceylon Civil Service and served in senior positions in the Ministries of Lands Industry and Planning and took early retirement in 1972 to establish and head the Marga Institute. He was consultant to the ILO UNCTAD, UNU, WHO and member of the Board of Trustees of the International Food Policy Research Institute. He is the recipient of several International and national awards including the Eisenhower Fellowship USA; the Health for All medal awarded by the WHO; the "Deshamanya" award for distinguished public service and D.Litt conferred by the University of Colombo in 1996. Has authored several books articles and publications on development issues. His involvement in the literary field includes the publication of the literary critical journal Harvest (1945) the first of its kind in Sri Lanka, short stories (“The Garden”, “Thief in the Night”) and literary critical articles (“Language without Metaphor”).Godfrey Gunatilleke is at present Chairman Emeritus and Senior Advisor of the Marga Institute.
